mnemonics: FIDELITY: From Idea to Launch, Every Detail Increases in Thoroughness and Yield.
keyPoints: Low-Fidelity: Focuses on structure and flow, often using simple sketches or wireframes.
Mid-Fidelity: Adds more detail and interactivity (e.g., grayscale wireframes with clickable elements) but omits final visuals.
High-Fidelity: Closely resembles the final product with detailed visuals, interactions, and content.
commonMistakes: Assuming high-fidelity is always better; low-fi is more efficient for testing concepts and flow early on.
Adding high-fidelity visuals too early, which can distract from feedback on core functionality and user experience.
